title: Mindset: The New Psychology of Success
author: Carol Dweck
content: The view you adopt for yourself profoundly affects the way you lead your life. If you believe that your qualities are unchangeable - the fixed mindset - you will want to prove yourself correct over and over rather than learning from your mistakes. If you believe that your qualities can be cultivated through your efforts - the growth mindset - you will thrive on the journey. The growth mindset is based on the belief that your basic qualities are things you can cultivate through your efforts. Although people may differ in every which way - in their initial talents and aptitudes, interests, or temperaments - everyone can change and grow through application and experience. The growth mindset does not deny the role of intelligence or talent, but it sees them as starting points that can be developed.
questions_raised: How does mindset affect learning and growth?
Can fixed traits be changed through effort?
What are the implications for helping others develop?
insights_gained: Effort and experience can change capabilities
